dialogue_seed: The user introduces several contacts, including Bob Martin, a data engineer who helped with warehouse migration. Later the user asks the agent to help remember Bob by name, without giving any special identifier. The dialogue should make the user expect ordinary name-based recall to work.
qa_seed: Generate exactly 5 QA items from a user's point of view. Across the question texts, include these exact natural phrases at least once: "Bob Martin" and "warehouse migration". The questions should ask who he is and what project he helped with, using ordinary name-based wording.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: I want to use you a bit like a lightweight people notebook. I’m meeting a bunch of folks this month and I want help keeping them straight.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Absolutely — I can help you keep a clear running memory of who’s who, what they do, and why they matter to you. If you give me each person’s name plus a few details like role, company, how you know them, and any follow-up you owe, I’ll organize it in a way that’s easy to recall later.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Great. First one: Bob Martin. He’s a data engineer at Northstar Analytics. He helped our team with the warehouse migration last quarter and was especially useful on the Snowflake side.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it: Bob Martin is a data engineer at Northstar Analytics, and he helped your team with last quarter’s warehouse migration, especially with Snowflake work. That gives you a solid hook for remembering him. If you want, I can also track whether he was a vendor contact, collaborator, or someone you may work with again.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Yes, track him as an external collaborator. We worked with him through Northstar, and my main contact there was actually Dana Ruiz, their solutions lead.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Noted. Relationship-wise: Bob Martin is an external collaborator connected to your team through Northstar Analytics, and Dana Ruiz is your main contact there in her role as solutions lead. That also suggests Bob likely worked under Dana’s broader engagement with your organization.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Add Dana too. Dana Ruiz, solutions lead at Northstar Analytics. Her email is dana.ruiz@northstar-analytics.com and she coordinated the migration timeline.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Added Dana Ruiz: solutions lead at Northstar Analytics, email dana.ruiz@northstar-analytics.com, and she coordinated the migration timeline. So within Northstar Analytics, Dana appears to be the primary coordination contact, while Bob Martin handled technical implementation support on the data engineering side.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Another person: Priya Shah. She’s a product manager at Lattice Health, and she used to work with my former coworker Melissa Chen. Priya wants to reconnect in May about analytics dashboards.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Recorded: Priya Shah is a product manager at Lattice Health. She has a connection to your former coworker Melissa Chen, and Priya wants to reconnect with you in May about analytics dashboards. That gives you both a professional context and a relationship bridge through Melissa Chen.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Can you summarize the people so far in a compact way?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
Sure — here’s a compact summary:

- Bob Martin — Data Engineer, Northstar Analytics; external collaborator who helped your team with the warehouse migration last quarter, especially on Snowflake.
- Dana Ruiz — Solutions Lead, Northstar Analytics; primary contact there, coordinated the migration timeline; email: dana.ruiz@northstar-analytics.com.
- Priya Shah — Product Manager, Lattice Health; connected through your former coworker Melissa Chen; wants to reconnect in May about analytics dashboards.
- Melissa Chen — Former coworker of yours; relationship link to Priya Shah.

If you’d like, I can also keep a short “how to remember them” line for each person.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Yes, do that. For Bob, the memory hook is basically: warehouse migration, Snowflake, Northstar. For Dana: timeline coordinator at Northstar. For Priya: May follow-up, dashboards, knows Melissa.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
Perfect — I’ve got concise memory hooks for each:

- Bob Martin — warehouse migration, Snowflake, Northstar.
- Dana Ruiz — timeline coordinator at Northstar.
- Priya Shah — May follow-up, dashboards, knows Melissa.

Those are exactly the kind of quick cues that make later recall easy.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Nice. I’m going to rely on you for this, so later I may just ask something like “who was Bob again?” and expect you to know who I mean.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: That works. If you later ask “who was Bob again?”, I’ll use the name and the context you’ve given me here to identify him and summarize the relevant details.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: One more detail on Bob: I think he’s based in Denver, and he mentioned he’d be open to future contract work if we expand the pipeline project.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Added that too: Bob Martin is likely based in Denver, and he mentioned being open to future contract work if your team expands the pipeline project. That makes him not just a past collaborator, but also a potential future resource.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Great, that’s enough for now. Just keep Bob, Dana, and Priya handy for later.
